home.title: Navykus is an international community of active schoolchildren
home.description: Navykus is an international educational platform for active schoolchildren: championships, project activities, mentors and team search.

blog.title: Blog — Navykus
blog.description: Community news, educational articles, interviews, participant stories and materials of the Navykus platform.

app.ca9bce21fd: Develop key skills for the future through online championships, create viable projects and connect with like-minded people around the world under the guidance of experienced experts.

app.db07934f76: International educational platform and community of active schoolchildren. We develop soft skills and launch global projects.

aboutprojectpage.3841022721: “The traditional school focuses on academic theory, while the modern world requires applied skills, developed design thinking and the ability to work in cross-cultural teams. »
aboutprojectpage.d1728b4c: Do you want to become part of the international community?
aboutprojectpage.ac15bd8cf7: We are creating the future of education by connecting active students, academic mentors and global companies into a single, supportive development ecosystem.
aboutprojectpage.05c82da787: Navykus was created as a bridge between school desks and real professional challenges. We believe that every student, regardless of geographic location, deserves access to world-class mentoring, cutting-edge design tools, and a global community of like-minded people.
aboutprojectpage.c2b671bade: Our community brings together guys from more than 15 countries, speaking different languages, but united by one passion - creating viable projects that change the world for the better.
aboutprojectpage.4d60e65bd8: Navykus is not just an educational site. It is an international launching pad, breaking down boundaries and creating a new generation of conscious leaders.
aboutprojectpage.31f9f3d409: Navykus brings together key participants in the educational ecosystem. Select your role to discover the benefits and take the first step towards collaboration.
aboutprojectpage.b23f0c7c43: What can you do at Navykus
aboutprojectpage.d5362b6b2b: The platform provides a continuous development cycle - from the first idea to the presentation of a working MVP.
aboutprojectpage.e6970f04e8: We have collected the most frequently asked questions from parents, students and mentors.
aboutprojectpage.7cea9cf73e: Don't put off your development. Register on the platform right now, find your dream team and start creating projects of the future.
aboutprojectpage.4af3da7a03: Solving real cases of large brands
aboutprojectpage.825d7c88f4: Formation of an international portfolio of projects
aboutprojectpage.93290f394a: Finding like-minded people and creating teams
aboutprojectpage.3f8c8b54eb: Increasing the school's rating due to student victories
aboutprojectpage.cba47258ca: Contribution to the development of global EdTech
aboutprojectpage.9c2513d649: Scouting talented guys for your laboratories
aboutprojectpage.d5dce53393: Exchange of experience with colleagues from MIT, Sorbonne and YC
aboutprojectpage.5767a5f856: Direct contact with the leaders of tomorrow
aboutprojectpage.fb3a394753: Solving corporate problems with the hands of creative schoolchildren
aboutprojectpage.7f6a7b6bbe: Branding among an active youth audience
aboutprojectpage.cc18b5c571: Implementation of corporate social responsibility initiatives
aboutprojectpage.9d86488fb6: Solve global cases in the field of ecology, urbanism, IT and diplomacy. Compete with students from other countries for grants and recognition.
aboutprojectpage.9915e585a3: Transform your bold ideas into working prototypes and startups using proven methods from the world's leading incubators.
aboutprojectpage.66695efbd6: Use our smart matching system to find collaborators, designers, coders, or join a promising initiative.
aboutprojectpage.c22bfce55b: Pitch your ideas to experts from MIT, Y Combinator, and leading corporations. High-quality feedback accelerates growth significantly.
aboutprojectpage.48d877aa9c: Attend webinars, master classes and workshops on programming, pitching, analytics and design absolutely free.
aboutprojectpage.20a478bc7a: Master critical thinking, systems approach, digital literacy and emotional intelligence in an applied format.
aboutprojectpage.4b5e0f1908: Who can become a participant in the project?
aboutprojectpage.09fd377d38: The project was created for active schoolchildren and students aged 10 to 22 years old who are interested in project activities, startups, technology, social innovation and international communication.
aboutprojectpage.a4f04e2aad: Is prior experience in hackathons required?
aboutprojectpage.3aeea8f6e3: Absolutely not! Our championships and programs are structured in such a way that they are comfortable for beginners to enter. Team trackers and webinars will help you master the basic design logic from scratch.
aboutprojectpage.ace0eadb3d: Can I register if I don't have a team?
aboutprojectpage.090808a2de: Yes, of course. More than half of our members register individually. Immediately after submitting an application, the platform will provide access to a closed community and chats, where you can easily assemble a team or join an existing one.
aboutprojectpage.1d5cd942b3: How does the team search process work?
aboutprojectpage.3e6cba422a: You can search for teammates through a special catalog of profiles on the platform, filtering the guys by competencies (designer, speaker, idea generator), or take part in a live pitch matching session before the start of the championship.
aboutprojectpage.55cd784afe: How do events and project defense work?
aboutprojectpage.b38dd282f8: All processes take place entirely online on our platform. Workshops and lectures are broadcast recorded and live, work is carried out in Miro/Figma, and projects are defended in front of a jury in Zoom or Discord.
aboutprojectpage.e663d62bb5: How can I contact the organizers of Navykus?
aboutprojectpage.b966ab17f5: You can always write to our 24-hour support service by email info@navykus.org or in the official Telegram account @navykus_com. We will answer any organizational or technical questions.

activitiespage.9bc3c6004a: Find lectures, workshops, project sprints, team formats, and online meetups that help build skills and real projects.
activitiespage.807368f2dd: Search by title, format, topic, or skills...
activitiespage.5f082a56: Don't know where to start? Determine your readiness level and choose the appropriate format.
activitiespage.08c342896d: There is no activity for this filter yet
activitiespage.4eb7854fc8: Start with a short format: lecture, master class or online meeting. It's free and will help you understand what is closest to you.
activitiespage.0ef507d3b1: Join a project activity or networking meeting. Gather a team and start a real project.
activitiespage.27354a5dbf: Go to the championship - a real challenge, an expert jury and international prizes await you there.
activitiespage.8469599d4c: Use a matching system. We will help you find like-minded people with interests and skills.
activitiespage.4dbca5f4b0: Fill out a short form and our coordinator will help you choose the format that suits you.

championshippage.75eb0d84: Can I participate if I don't have a team?
championshippage.ccc076b0: Is there a fee to participate? And what are the requirements for technology?
championshippage.0a5a7de0: How much time per day will the cup take up?
championshippage.fdb6874f: Can students from other countries participate?
championshippage.c264c842: What document will I receive as a result of participation?
championshippage.47dfb4af: Where and when will the results be published?
championshippage.3015b9f8: Without a team? We will solve this problem!
championshippage.d0dd2427: Do you have a team to participate?
championshippage.06f875a6: How many people are on your team (including you)?
championshippage.bacaeee8: Why do you want to participate? What benefits do you want to receive?
championshippage.567335aef2: Developing innovative solutions to the urban challenges of the future. International jury, real cases from urbanists, architects and ecologists from all over the world.
championshippage.dd865f6e85: Solve a global urban case, assemble an international dream team, and defend your solution to mentors from MIT and Smart Cities experts.
championshippage.8de641ff48: Schoolchildren and students of grades 8–11, passionate about ecology, IT, urbanism and design.
championshippage.14fe253652: International online championship (Group work in Miro/Figma, presentation of solutions in Zoom)
championshippage.ed233d55cc: Individually or in a team (from 2 to 5 people)
championshippage.034bb56718: Interactive project/presentation on urban space renovation, carbon footprint reduction plan or IT solution for a smart city.
championshippage.49a254c0ad: Technical and economic feasibility of the idea (25%)
championshippage.c3ab1eab18: Depth of problem analysis and elaboration of details (25%)
championshippage.bdbee4fdad: Quality of defense, presentation design and team spirit (25%)
championshippage.61fb774e62: Innovative recycling and sorting of household waste
championshippage.058159276f: Alternative energy sources in residential areas
championshippage.f1d7d23827: Intelligent IT solutions for public transport
championshippage.001a2ebaa3: Professor of Urban Studies at MIT
championshippage.bdea62169b: Design of public spaces, participatory design
championshippage.bc7f7951f3: Chief Analyst at the University of Rotterdam
championshippage.1c03d08986: Environmental audit and circular economy of cities
championshippage.7b04173c40: Please enter the correct age (from 10 to 24 years old)
championshippage.a79744eacf: Enter your contact for quick communication (Telegram or WhatsApp)
championshippage.4ee6afa1a6: Fill out the form on this page, indicate your interests and select the participation format (individual or team).
championshippage.c2761e4600: If you don't have a team, our smart platform will automatically offer you like-minded teammates or give you access to a private chat.
championshippage.bf30acbb04: On the start day, teams receive access to their personal account with detailed technical specifications from partner companies.
championshippage.a1f7854119: You design a solution in Miro/Figma, attend daily webinars from speakers, and improve the prototype based on instructions.
championshippage.d81bad3646: Each team is assigned a personal expert (tracker), who conducts a review session and helps eliminate errors.
championshippage.20dc81369a: Teams present their presentations and prototypes to an international jury live online.
championshippage.1c3fdab6c1: Protection: up to 5 minutes per team
championshippage.f34151eece: The jury assigns points, announces winners and runners-up, and each participant receives a personal certificate and detailed feedback.
championshippage.49424077d7: Certificate in portfolio for all participants
championshippage.4b587e32b1: Yes! More than half of the participants register one at a time. We have provided an interactive matching process, live zoom meetings for getting to know each other and a special catalog of questionnaires in which you can easily assemble a team 2 days before the start.
championshippage.abf0a3c0da: Participation is absolutely free. All you need is a stable internet connection, access to Zoom/Discord, and basic skills in Google Slides or Miro/Figma. There is no need to install any complex software.
championshippage.842ede64f5: This usually takes 2-3 hours of free time a day. Workshops take place outside of school hours (in the evening Moscow time) and are recorded, so you can easily combine the cup with lessons at school or in pairs at college.
championshippage.c00db4e511: Yes, the championship is international and is held entirely online. We are waiting for guys from the CIS, Europe, Asia and America. The main working language of the championship is Russian, but project defense and materials are also available in English.
championshippage.75488dd388: Each participant who has reached the defense stage and sent a solution receives an official international participant certificate with the signature of the organizers and the logos of our partners, which verifies the acquired hard and soft skills for your portfolio.
championshippage.59016ab8e3: The results and scores will be published live at the closing ceremony, as well as duplicated on the platform and sent to all participants in their personal accounts and email within 24 hours after the end of the defense.
championshippage.f30417ddf0: Understand real environmental and urban challenges
championshippage.1d22704f2f: It doesn't matter if you know how to write code or draw presentations. The Cup is designed so that each participant reveals their strengths.
championshippage.9913df95c5: The Cup provides equal chances for guys with any level of training. We provide training workshops, templates and mentoring support to ensure your first project turns out professional.
championshippage.7f6fbf84a6: “Try to distribute roles in the team: one strong speaker, one idea generator, one slide designer and one analyst. This division of labor guarantees the best marks from the jury!”
championshippage.3cb46975e1: Don't be afraid to sign up one at a time. Navykus is primarily a networking platform. We will organize a system for automatically selecting teams based on your interests and competencies.
championshippage.9dee187e5f: Live Zoom dating session before the start
championshippage.a3e6b0b4fd: Register in an individual format, in the application form select Looking for a team.” Immediately after this you will receive a link to a closed community with more than 100 co-authors.
championshippage.fe12153d63: Solving cases is not programming. This is primarily the study of people, the generation of vivid metaphors, the creation of beautiful visual schemes and the telling of captivating stories.
championshippage.c174c67149: “Cases on Sustainable Cities consist of 50% understanding of the psychology of citizens and the design of spaces. Be creative and offer a non-standard visual solution!”
championshippage.1d5616ef28: Make yourself known to global EdTech leaders
championshippage.fae9e0f0cb: Our cup is visited by scouts from international universities and HR specialists from large companies. This is your chance to receive a paid internship or letter of recommendation.
championshippage.26ed71e452: The main prize of the cup is mentoring support for the startup until its full release and letters of recommendation from MIT professors and Y Combinator trackers.
championshippage.73c7822daa: We have developed a clear step-by-step online route. Click on the steps to explore details and important markers.
championshippage.642a217314: Your projects will be assessed by professors from leading universities around the world and practitioners from international corporations. The composition is managed through a CMS system in real time.
championshippage.5788077ace: Complete the simple form below to reserve your place. We will process the questionnaire and send further instructions by email.
championshippage.06c9f79607: We have reserved a place in the cup participants' grid. A letter with a link to your personal account and instructions has been sent to your email address.
championshippage.0e95df1685: Show the ticket to the curator on Telegram:
championshippage.949a42b4b3: Since you registered without a team, we advise you to go to our scenario matching section, where you can fill out a competency card and join other active guys.
championshippage.098019329a: Unfortunately, accepting new entries for the current Sustainable Cities Championship is temporarily suspended or closed due to the limit of participants being reached.
championshippage.9c2f9e9c80: No, I'm looking for a team 🤝
championshippage.0149330653: Tell us a little about yourself and your goals for the cup
championshippage.b21fd1fe62: By clicking the button, you agree to the terms of storage of personal data and Navykus regulations.
championshippage.79e49fa24f: places for the qualifying stage. Hurry up to submit your application before the registration form closes.

findteampage.3db06b0fab: Questionnaires are reviewed before publication. We guarantee that the data is verified.

findteampage.44b960a4ba: Leave your profile and other students will find you. Fill out the form in 2 minutes and join the international community.
findteampage.a264de7b48: Click the “Submit your application” button in the header of the site or anywhere on this page. Fill out the form: name, age, country, interests, skills and contacts. The questionnaire will appear after verification by a moderator.

findteampage.b734b04eb7: Let the coordinator know - we will hide the profile from the general list so as not to receive unnecessary messages. You can always return it later.
findteampage.a80b8b09d5: Use the contacts specified in the form (Telegram or email). Do not share personal information (address, school, passport) and do not click on suspicious links. If in doubt, contact the organizers.

tournaments.title: Navykus Global Case Cup: Sustainable Cities
tournaments.description: Developing innovative solutions to the urban challenges of the future. International jury, real cases from urbanists, architects and ecologists from all over the world.
tournaments.suitableFor: Schoolchildren in grades 8–11 who are passionate about ecology, urbanism and social innovation.
tournaments.format: Online (group stage, defense of projects via video conference)

tournaments.title: Future Tech Project Incubator
tournaments.description: An intensive program to launch your own startup or social project. Turn your idea into a minimum viable product (MVP) in three weeks with guidance from Silicon Valley trackers.
tournaments.suitableFor: Authors of ideas, aspiring programmers, designers and future entrepreneurs.
tournaments.format: Online (intensive workshops, traction maps, demo day)

tournaments.title: International Youth Debate & Policy Forum
tournaments.description: Global debate on the ethics of artificial intelligence and space exploration. Develop skills in argumentation and diplomatic correspondence in English and Russian.
tournaments.suitableFor: Students developing public speaking skills, humanities and international relations.

pillars.label: 01 / CHAMPIONSHIPS
pillars.title: Online championships
pillars.description: Large-scale international competitions in solving cases. Try yourself in solving real problems from industry leaders.

pillars.label: 02 / COMMUNITY
pillars.title: Community and Mentors
pillars.description: Space for matching and expert mentoring. Find teammates from all over the world and get direct contact with practitioners from top universities.

pillars.label: 03 / ACTIVITIES
pillars.title: Useful activities
pillars.description: Master classes, workshops, open lectures from experts. Continuous learning regardless of participation in tournaments.

experts.name: Dr. Mark Spielman
experts.role: Professor of Urban Studies at MIT
experts.description: 15+ years of research in the field of environmental technologies and automation of urban infrastructure. Mentor of the Sustainable Cities track.

experts.name: Alex Chen
experts.description: Founder of two EdTech startups. Helps students crystallize chaotic ideas into a clear, working prototype.

experts.name: Anna Sokolova
experts.description: Coordinator of global youth forums with the support of UNESCO. Teaches effective presentation and the art of debate.

scenarios.title: Participate in the championship
scenarios.who: Schoolchild 14–18 years old with or without an idea
scenarios.why: Wants to test his strength in solving cases, receive awards and certificates. Can submit a form to the talent database for matching for upcoming tournaments.

scenarios.title: Find a team
scenarios.who: Author of the idea or solo participant
scenarios.why: The author needs designers and developers to implement the MVP; a loner needs a strong team to join.

trustPoints.title: Global scale
trustPoints.description: Participants and mentors from more than 15 countries create a truly multicultural educational environment.

trustPoints.title: Real practical cases
trustPoints.description: No abstract tasks. All cases and programs were created in collaboration with existing businesses and scientific laboratories.

trustPoints.title: Direct contact with mentors
trustPoints.description: Live communication, regular feedback, debriefing and expert assessment are the key value of our programs.

trustPoints.title: Turnkey project work
trustPoints.description: From a hypothesis and search for a problem to the first working prototype and defense to potential investors.

trustPoints.title: Finding like-minded people
trustPoints.description: A reliable and supportive community of ambitious peers who motivate you to move forward.

activities.title: Workshop: How to create an MVP in 48 hours
activities.shortDescription: Learn to turn ideas into a working prototype using no-code tools and design thinking.
activities.fullDescription: A two-day intensive workshop where you will go through the full cycle of creating a minimum viable product: from idea generation to presentation. You will learn how to use Figma, Notion and modern no-code platforms for rapid prototyping. At the end, each participant will receive a ready-made template for their MVP and feedback from practicing product managers.
activities.who: Schoolchildren aged 12–18 interested in product development and startups
benefits: Ready-made MVP prototype of your own project
Skills in working with Figma and no-code tools
activities.prerequisites: Basic understanding of what MVP is. No experience required.

activities.title: Lecture: Artificial intelligence in education
activities.shortDescription: An expert from Stanford will tell you how AI is changing school education and what skills will be in demand.
activities.fullDescription: An open lecture from a guest speaker - a researcher in the field of EdTech and artificial intelligence. You will learn how machine learning is already personalizing the educational process, what ethical issues the introduction of AI in schools raises, and what schoolchildren need to do to remain in the ranks of those who control technology, and not vice versa. After the lecture there is a question and answer session.
benefits: An Expert Perspective from a Stanford Researcher

activities.title: Hackathon: Green technologies for the city
activities.shortDescription: Team hackathon to develop environmental solutions for smart cities.
activities.fullDescription: A 48-hour team hackathon in which teams of 2-5 people must design a green solution for urban infrastructure. You will work in Miro and Figma, consult with environmental mentors and present the final pitch to the jury. The best teams will receive an invitation to the Future Tech Project Incubator.
activities.who: Schoolchildren aged 14–19, passionate about ecology, IT and urbanism
benefits: Invitation to the incubator for the best
activities.prerequisites: Desire to work in a team. Any skill is welcome.

activities.title: Master class: Public speaking and pitching
activities.shortDescription: How to convince the jury of the value of your project in 5 minutes - from structure to presentation.
activities.fullDescription: A practical master class from a tracker who helped schoolchildren win international debates and pitch sessions. You will understand the structure of an ideal pitch, practice speaking in pairs and receive personal feedback. It is especially useful for those who are preparing to defend a project at a championship or want to develop public speaking skills.
activities.who: Schoolchildren aged 13–18, ready to defend projects
activities.prerequisites: It is advisable to have your own project or idea

activities.title: Team track: Design sprint for social projects
activities.shortDescription: A 5-day design sprint where teams create a social project from scratch.
activities.fullDescription: Intensive team program using the design sprint methodology: day 1 - understanding the problem, day 2 - generating ideas, day 3 - decision making, day 4 - prototyping, day 5 - testing and presentation. Each team will receive its own facilitator and will present the result to an expert jury. Ideal for those who want to try teamwork in a structured format.
activities.who: Schoolchildren aged 14–18, looking for a team or ready to form their own
benefits: Full design sprint cycle on a real project
activities.prerequisites: Desire to work in a team. No experience required.

activities.title: Online conference: Future of Skills 2025
activities.shortDescription: Three-day conference on key skills for generation Z.
activities.fullDescription: The largest online conference of the Navykus community: 3 days, 20+ speakers from MIT, YC, Sorbonne, UN. Topics: critical thinking, emotional intelligence, digital literacy, global citizenship. Every day there is a keynote speech, panel discussion and interactive session. The conference took place in December 2025 and brought together 800+ participants from 30 countries.
benefits: Recordings of all reports are available

activities.title: Project: Peer Mentoring Program
activities.shortDescription: Become a mentor for younger students or find your own mentor.
activities.fullDescription: The platform allows older schoolchildren (16–19 years old) to become mentors for younger students (12–15 years old) and help them with project activities, choosing areas of development and preparing for participation in championships. The program includes onboarding for mentors, regular meetings and coordinator support. For mentees, this is an opportunity to get a personal mentor from another country.
activities.date: Applications can be submitted until September 1, 2026
benefits: Menti: personal mentor from another country
activities.prerequisites: For mentors - experience of participation in Navykusa projects

activities.title: Workshop: Research Fundamentals
activities.shortDescription: How to conduct research, work with data, and write scientific articles for schoolchildren.
activities.fullDescription: A three-hour workshop on research methods relevant to schoolchildren: qualitative and quantitative methods, working with sources, basic statistics, citation ethics and the structure of a scientific article. The speaker is a university lecturer who specializes in working with school research projects. As a result, a practical task with feedback.

activities.title: Hackathon: Accessible Environment 2025
activities.shortDescription: Development of IT solutions for people with disabilities.
activities.fullDescription: The annual hackathon of the Navykus community, dedicated to the development of technological solutions to ensure an accessible environment. The teams worked on real-life issues of inclusion: navigation apps for the visually impaired, interfaces for people with motor impairments, game mechanics for neurodivergent children. The final defense took place with the participation of experts from international foundations.
activities.who: Schoolchildren 14–19 years old, developers, designers, activists
benefits: A real product for an inclusive environment

activities.title: Networking: Asian Youth Connect
activities.shortDescription: Meeting for participants from Asia - search for teams and exchange of experience.
activities.fullDescription: Specialized networking session for schoolchildren from Asia and neighboring regions. In 2 hours, participants get to know each other in a virtual speed-dating format, share ideas, and look for teammates for the upcoming championships. The language of communication is English. The coordinator helps form mixed international teams.
activities.who: Schoolchildren aged 13–18 from the Asian region
benefits: Search for a team for the championship

teamMembers.name: Alisa Rashidova
teamMembers.shortBio: I am interested in product design and UX research. I am learning to apply design thinking to solve real problems in ecology and urbanism.
teamMembers.targetProject: Navykus Global Case Cup: Sustainable Cities
teamMembers.whyLooking: I want to find a team of developers and analysts to create an MVP project for sustainable cities. I have experience participating in 2 design sprints.

teamMembers.name: Aidar Sultanov
teamMembers.shortBio: Programmer and AI enthusiast. I write in Python and JavaScript, participated in computer science Olympiads. I'm looking for a team for a project incubator.
teamMembers.whyLooking: I have an idea for an EdTech startup, but I need a team - a designer and a business analyst. Ready to take on the technical part.

teamMembers.name: Miya Sato
teamMembers.shortBio: I participate in debates in English and Japanese. I am interested in global AI ethics and space law. I want to unite the team for an international forum.
teamMembers.whyLooking: Looking for members with a technical background for the AI ethics team. I have experience organizing school debates.

teamMembers.name: Artyom Volkov
teamMembers.shortBio: Aspiring developer with an interest in web technologies and data visualization. I participate in a school scientific circle on ecology.
teamMembers.targetProject: Navykus Global Case Cup: Sustainable Cities
teamMembers.whyLooking: I'm looking for a team for a case championship in urban planning. I can implement a dashboard to visualize the city’s environmental data.

teamMembers.name: Nurlahon Karimova
teamMembers.shortBio: Environmental activist at school. Organized a recycling program. I want to scale the project to the international level.
teamMembers.whyLooking: I am looking for developers and researchers to scale up eco-audits. I want to create an application for monitoring the environmental performance of a school.

teamMembers.name: David Kovalenko
teamMembers.shortBio: Future entrepreneur. I study product management and startup ecosystem. Participated in 3 hackathons.
teamMembers.whyLooking: I'm looking for a technical team to launch an MVP. I have a proven hypothesis in the HealthTech space. I am involved in product strategy myself.

teamMembers.name: Fatima Al-Khalid
teamMembers.shortBio: I write scientific articles on biotechnology. Participating in online research with a university laboratory. I'm looking for an interdisciplinary team.
teamMembers.targetProject: Navykus Global Case Cup: Sustainable Cities
teamMembers.whyLooking: I want to apply biotechnological approaches to urban problems. I'm looking for a team that can combine different disciplines.

teamMembers.name: Boris Nguyen
teamMembers.shortBio: Finalist of the national robotics championship. I develop ROS applications and study computer vision. I'm looking for a team for a social project.
teamMembers.targetProject: Team track: Design sprint for social projects
teamMembers.whyLooking: I am looking for a designer and researcher for a robot assistant project for people with disabilities. There is a working prototype.
